root_text.an11.16:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ānando vesāliyaṁ viharati beluvagāmake.
root_text.an11.16:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena dasamo gahapati aṭṭhakanāgaro pāṭaliputtaṁ anuppatto hoti kenacideva karaṇīyena.
root_text.an11.16:2.1: Atha kho dasamo gahapati aṭṭhakanāgaro yena kukkuṭārāmo yena aññataro bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an11.16:2.2: “kahaṁ nu kho, bhante, āyasmā ānando etarahi viharati?
root_text.an11.16:2.3: Dassanakāmā hi mayaṁ, bhante, āyasmantaṁ ānandan”ti.
root_text.an11.16:2.4: “Eso, gahapati, āyasmā ānando vesāliyaṁ viharati beluvagāmake”ti.
root_text.an11.16:3.1: Atha kho dasamo gahapati aṭṭhakanāgaro pāṭaliputte taṁ karaṇīyaṁ tīretvā yena vesālī beluvagāmako yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho dasamo gahapati aṭṭhakanāgaro āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an11.16:3.2: “atthi nu kho, bhante ānanda, tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena ekadhammo sammadakkhāto, yattha bhikkhuno appamattassa ātāpino pahitattassa viharato avimuttaṁ vā cittaṁ vimuccati, aparikkhīṇā vā āsavā parikkhayaṁ gacchanti, ananuppattaṁ vā anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ anupāpuṇātī”ti?
root_text.an11.16:3.3: “Atthi kho, gahapati, tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena ekadhammo sammadakkhāto, yattha bhikkhuno appamattassa ātāpino pahitattassa viharato avimuttaṁ vā cittaṁ vimuccati, aparikkhīṇā vā āsavā parikkhayaṁ gacchanti, ananuppattaṁ vā anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ anupāpuṇātī”ti.
root_text.an11.16:4.1: “Katamo pana, bhante ānanda, tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena ekadhammo sammadakkhāto, yattha bhikkhuno appamattassa ātāpino pahitattassa viharato avimuttaṁ vā cittaṁ vimuccati, aparikkhīṇā vā āsavā parikkhayaṁ gacchanti, ananuppattaṁ vā anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ anupāpuṇātī”ti?
root_text.an11.16:4.2: “Idha, gahapati, bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an11.16:4.5: ‘Yaṁ kho pana kiñci abhisaṅkhataṁ abhisañcetayitaṁ, tadaniccaṁ nirodhadhamman’ti pajānāti.
root_text.an11.16:4.6: So tattha ṭhito āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇāti;
root_text.an11.16:4.7: no ce āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇāti, teneva dhammarāgena tāya dhammanandiyā pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā opapātiko hoti tattha parinibbāyī anāvattidhammo tasmā lokā.
root_text.an11.16:4.8: Ayampi kho, gahapati, tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena ekadhammo sammadakkhāto, yattha bhikkhuno appamattassa ātāpino pahitattassa viharato avimuttaṁ vā cittaṁ vimuccati, aparikkhīṇā vā āsavā parikkhayaṁ gacchanti, ananuppattaṁ vā anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ anupāpuṇāti.
root_text.an11.16:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapati, bhikkhu vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā ajjhattaṁ sampasādanaṁ cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ avitakkaṁ avicāraṁ samādhijaṁ pītisukhaṁ dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ …pe…
root_text.an11.16:5.6: ‘Yaṁ kho pana kiñci abhisaṅkhataṁ abhisañcetayitaṁ tadaniccaṁ nirodhadhamman’ti pajānāti.
root_text.an11.16:5.7: So tattha ṭhito āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇāti;
root_text.an11.16:5.8: no ce āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇāti, teneva dhammarāgena tāya dhammanandiyā pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā opapātiko hoti tattha parinibbāyī anāvattidhammo tasmā lokā.
root_text.an11.16:5.9: Ayampi kho, gahapati, tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena ekadhammo sammadakkhāto, yattha bhikkhuno appamattassa ātāpino pahitattassa viharato avimuttaṁ vā cittaṁ vimuccati aparikkhīṇā vā āsavā parikkhayaṁ gacchanti, ananuppattaṁ vā anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ anupāpuṇāti.
root_text.an11.16:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapati, bhikkhu mettāsahagatena cetasā ekaṁ disaṁ pharitvā viharati tathā dutiyaṁ tathā tatiyaṁ tathā catutthaṁ. Iti uddhamadho tiriyaṁ sabbadhi sabbattatāya sabbāvantaṁ lokaṁ mettāsahagatena cetasā ekaṁ disaṁ pharitvā viharati vipulena mahaggatena appamāṇena averena abyāpajjena pharitvā viharati.
root_text.an11.16:6.4: ‘Yaṁ kho pana kiñci abhisaṅkhataṁ abhisañcetayitaṁ tadaniccaṁ nirodhadhamman’ti pajānāti.
root_text.an11.16:6.5: So tattha ṭhito āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇāti;
root_text.an11.16:6.6: no ce āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇāti, teneva dhammarāgena tāya dhammanandiyā pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā opapātiko hoti tattha parinibbāyī anāvattidhammo tasmā lokā.
root_text.an11.16:6.7: Ayampi kho, gahapati, tena bhagavatā jānatā …pe… ananuppattaṁ vā anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ anupāpuṇāti.
root_text.an11.16:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapati, bhikkhu karuṇāsahagatena cetasā …pe…
root_text.an11.16:7.3: upekkhāsahagatena cetasā ekaṁ disaṁ pharitvā viharati tathā dutiyaṁ tathā tatiyaṁ tathā catutthaṁ.
root_text.an11.16:7.4: Iti uddhamadho tiriyaṁ sabbadhi sabbattatāya sabbāvantaṁ lokaṁ upekkhāsahagatena cetasā vipulena mahaggatena appamāṇena averena abyāpajjena pharitvā viharati.
root_text.an11.16:7.7: ‘Yaṁ kho pana kiñci abhisaṅkhataṁ abhisañcetayitaṁ tadaniccaṁ nirodhadhamman’ti pajānāti.
root_text.an11.16:7.8: So tattha ṭhito āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇāti;
root_text.an11.16:7.9: no ce āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇāti, teneva dhammarāgena tāya dhammanandiyā pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā opapātiko hoti tattha parinibbāyī anāvattidhammo tasmā lokā.
root_text.an11.16:7.10: Ayampi kho, gahapati, tena bhagavatā jānatā …pe… ananuppattaṁ vā anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ anupāpuṇāti.
root_text.an11.16:8.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapati, bhikkhu sabbaso rūpasaññānaṁ samatikkamā paṭighasaññānaṁ atthaṅgamā nānattasaññānaṁ amanasikārā ‘ananto ākāso’ti ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an11.16:8.4: ‘Yaṁ kho pana kiñci abhisaṅkhataṁ abhisañcetayitaṁ tadaniccaṁ nirodhadhamman’ti pajānāti.
root_text.an11.16:8.5: So tattha ṭhito āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇāti;
root_text.an11.16:8.6: no ce āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇāti, teneva dhammarāgena tāya dhammanandiyā pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā opapātiko hoti tattha parinibbāyī anāvattidhammo tasmā lokā.
root_text.an11.16:8.7: Ayampi kho, gahapati, tena bhagavatā jānatā …pe… ananuppattaṁ vā anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ anupāpuṇāti.
root_text.an11.16:9.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapati, bhikkhu sabbaso ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma ‘anantaṁ viññāṇan’ti viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati …pe…
root_text.an11.16:9.2: sabbaso viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma ‘natthi kiñcī’ti ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an11.16:9.5: ‘Yaṁ kho pana kiñci abhisaṅkhataṁ abhisañcetayitaṁ tadaniccaṁ nirodhadhamman’ti pajānāti.
root_text.an11.16:9.6: So tattha ṭhito āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇāti;
root_text.an11.16:9.7: no ce āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇāti, teneva dhammarāgena tāya dhammanandiyā pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā opapātiko hoti tattha parinibbāyī anāvattidhammo tasmā lokā.
root_text.an11.16:9.8: Ayampi kho, gahapati, tena bhagavatā jānatā …pe… ananuppattaṁ vā anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ anupāpuṇātī”ti.
root_text.an11.16:10.1: Evaṁ vutte dasamo gahapati aṭṭhakanāgaro āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an11.16:10.2: “seyyathāpi, bhante ānanda, puriso ekaṁ nidhimukhaṁ gavesanto sakideva ekādasa nidhimukhāni adhigaccheyya;
root_text.an11.16:10.3: evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, ekaṁ amatadvāraṁ gavesanto sakideva ekādasa amatadvārāni alatthaṁ sevanāya.
root_text.an11.16:10.4: Seyyathāpi, bhante, purisassa agāraṁ ekādasa dvāraṁ. So tasmiṁ agāre āditte ekamekenapi dvārena sakkuṇeyya attānaṁ sotthiṁ kātuṁ;
root_text.an11.16:10.5: evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, imesaṁ ekādasannaṁ amatadvārānaṁ ekamekenapi amatadvārena sakkuṇissāmi attānaṁ sotthiṁ kātuṁ.
root_text.an11.16:10.6: Ime hi nāma, bhante, aññatitthiyā ācariyassa ācariyadhanaṁ pariyesissanti.
root_text.an11.16:10.7: Kiṁ panāhaṁ āyasmato ānandassa pūjaṁ na karissāmī”ti.
root_text.an11.16:11.1: Atha kho dasamo gahapati aṭṭhakanāgaro vesālikañca pāṭaliputtakañca bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena sahatthā santappesi sampavāresi.
root_text.an11.16:11.2: Ekamekañca bhikkhuṁ paccekaṁ dussayugena acchādesi, āyasmantañca ānandaṁ ticīvarena.

translation_text.an11.16:1.1: At one time Venerable Ānanda was staying near Vesālī in the little village of Beluva.
translation_text.an11.16:1.2: Now at that time the householder Dasama, a wealthy citizen, had arrived at Pāṭaliputta on some business.
translation_text.an11.16:2.1: He went to the Chicken Monastery, approached a certain mendicant, and said to him,
translation_text.an11.16:2.2: “Sir, where is Venerable Ānanda now staying?
translation_text.an11.16:2.3: For I want to see him.”
translation_text.an11.16:2.4: “Householder, Venerable Ānanda is staying near Vesālī in the little village of Beluva.”
translation_text.an11.16:3.1: Then the householder Dasama, having concluded his business there, went to the little village of Beluva in Vesālī to see Ānanda. He bowed, sat down to one side, and said to Ānanda:
translation_text.an11.16:3.2: “Honorable Ānanda, is there one thing that has been rightly explained by the Blessed One—who knows and sees, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha—practicing which a diligent, keen, and resolute mendicant’s mind is freed, their defilements are ended, and they arrive at the supreme sanctuary from the yoke?”
translation_text.an11.16:4.2: “Householder, it’s when a mendicant, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters and remains in the first absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of seclusion, while placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.an11.16:4.4: ‘Even this first absorption is produced by choices and intentions.’
translation_text.an11.16:4.5: They understand: ‘But whatever is produced by choices and intentions is impermanent and liable to cessation.’
translation_text.an11.16:4.6: Abiding in that they attain the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an11.16:4.7: If they don’t attain the ending of defilements, with the ending of the five lower fetters they’re reborn spontaneously, because of their passion and love for that meditation. They are extinguished there, and are not liable to return from that world.
translation_text.an11.16:4.8: This is one thing that has been rightly explained by the Blessed One—who knows and sees, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha—practicing which a diligent, keen, and resolute mendicant’s mind is freed, their defilements are ended, and they arrive at the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.an11.16:5.1: Furthermore, as the placing of the mind and keeping it connected are stilled, they enter and remain in the second absorption …
translation_text.an11.16:5.5: ‘Even this fourth absorption is produced by choices and intentions.’
translation_text.an11.16:5.6: They understand: ‘But whatever is produced by choices and intentions is impermanent and liable to cessation.’
translation_text.an11.16:5.7: Abiding in that they attain the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an11.16:5.8: If they don’t attain the ending of defilements, with the ending of the five lower fetters they’re reborn spontaneously, because of their passion and love for that meditation. They are extinguished there, and are not liable to return from that world.
translation_text.an11.16:5.9: This too is one thing that has been rightly explained by the Blessed One—who knows and sees, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha—practicing which a diligent, keen, and resolute mendicant’s mind is freed, their defilements are ended, and they arrive at the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.an11.16:6.1: Furthermore, a mendicant meditates spreading a heart full of love to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth. In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of love to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will.
translation_text.an11.16:6.3: ‘Even this heart’s release by love is produced by choices and intentions.’
translation_text.an11.16:6.4: They understand: ‘But whatever is produced by choices and intentions is impermanent and liable to cessation.’
translation_text.an11.16:6.5: Abiding in that they attain the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an11.16:6.6: If they don’t attain the ending of defilements, with the ending of the five lower fetters they’re reborn spontaneously, because of their passion and love for that meditation. They are extinguished there, and are not liable to return from that world.
translation_text.an11.16:6.7: This too is one thing that has been rightly explained by the Blessed One …
translation_text.an11.16:7.1: Furthermore, a mendicant meditates spreading a heart full of compassion …
translation_text.an11.16:7.2: They meditate spreading a heart full of rejoicing …
translation_text.an11.16:7.3: They meditate spreading a heart full of equanimity to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth.
translation_text.an11.16:7.4: In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of equanimity to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will.
translation_text.an11.16:7.6: ‘Even this heart’s release by equanimity is produced by choices and intentions.’
translation_text.an11.16:7.7: They understand: ‘But whatever is produced by choices and intentions is impermanent and liable to cessation.’
translation_text.an11.16:7.8: Abiding in that they attain the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an11.16:7.9: If they don’t attain the ending of defilements, with the ending of the five lower fetters they’re reborn spontaneously, because of their passion and love for that meditation. They are extinguished there, and are not liable to return from that world.
translation_text.an11.16:7.10: This too is one thing that has been rightly explained by the Blessed One …
translation_text.an11.16:8.1: Furthermore, a mendicant, going totally beyond perceptions of form, with the disappearance of perceptions of impingement, not focusing on perceptions of diversity, aware that ‘space is infinite’, enters and remains in the dimension of infinite space.
translation_text.an11.16:8.3: ‘Even this attainment of the dimension of infinite space is produced by choices and intentions.’
translation_text.an11.16:8.4: They understand: ‘But whatever is produced by choices and intentions is impermanent and liable to cessation.’
translation_text.an11.16:8.5: Abiding in that they attain the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an11.16:8.6: If they don’t attain the ending of defilements, with the ending of the five lower fetters they’re reborn spontaneously, because of their passion and love for that meditation. They are extinguished there, and are not liable to return from that world.
translation_text.an11.16:8.7: This too is one thing that has been rightly explained by the Blessed One …
translation_text.an11.16:9.1: Furthermore, a mendicant, going totally beyond the dimension of infinite space, aware that ‘consciousness is infinite’, enters and remains in the dimension of infinite consciousness. …
translation_text.an11.16:9.2: Going totally beyond the dimension of infinite consciousness, aware that ‘there is nothing at all’, they enter and remain in the dimension of nothingness. …
translation_text.an11.16:9.4: ‘Even this attainment of the dimension of nothingness is produced by choices and intentions.’
translation_text.an11.16:9.5: They understand: ‘But whatever is produced by choices and intentions is impermanent and liable to cessation.’
translation_text.an11.16:9.6: Abiding in that they attain the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an11.16:9.7: If they don’t attain the ending of defilements, with the ending of the five lower fetters they’re reborn spontaneously, because of their passion and love for that meditation. They are extinguished there, and are not liable to return from that world.
translation_text.an11.16:9.8: This too is one thing that has been rightly explained by the Blessed One—who knows and sees, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha—practicing which a diligent, keen, and resolute mendicant’s mind is freed, their defilements are ended, and they reach the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.”
translation_text.an11.16:10.1: When he said this, the householder Dasama said to Venerable Ānanda:
translation_text.an11.16:10.2: “Honorable Ānanda, suppose a person was looking for the entrance to a treasure trove. And all at once they’d come across eleven entrances!
translation_text.an11.16:10.3: In the same way, I was searching for the door to freedom from death. And all at once I found eleven doors to freedom from death for cultivation.
translation_text.an11.16:10.4: Suppose a person had a house with eleven doors. If the house caught fire they’d be able to flee to safety through any one of those doors.
translation_text.an11.16:10.5: In the same way, I’m able to flee to safety through any one of these eleven doors to freedom from death.
translation_text.an11.16:10.6: Sir, those of other religions will seek a fee for the tutor.
translation_text.an11.16:10.7: Why shouldn’t I make an offering to Venerable Ānanda?”
translation_text.an11.16:11.1: Then the householder Dasama, having assembled the Saṅgha from Vesālī and Pāṭaliputta, served and satisfied them with his own hands with delicious fresh and cooked foods.
translation_text.an11.16:11.2: He clothed each and every mendicant in a pair of garments, with a set of three robes for Ānanda.
translation_text.an11.16:11.3: And he had a dwelling worth five hundred built for Ānanda.
